title: A cozier dev tool with a real data browser
summary: The in-app dev tools (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+D) are reorganized around four high-signal panels — Data, Changes, Logs, and Performance — with everything else tucked into a grouped "More" menu and a ⌘⇧P command palette, so the bottom strip is no longer a wall of tabs.
highlights: Data panel: query and browse every node in the real database grid, with schema-aware columns, live updates, and a query-plan inspector
Logs panel: flip the sync/SQLite/query/boot/trace debug channels and read captured output in-app
Performance panel: cold-start boot timeline, live frame budget and memory, storage stats, and recent traces in one place
Jump to any panel by name with the ⌘⇧P command palette
